title: Expose after in the CLI: --after on create/update and display in tk show
description:
Wire the new After field ([]string, json "after,omitempty", added to internal/tick/tick.go by a prior tick) through the CLI. Semantics: after = preferred ordering only, never gates readiness (that is blocked_by).

Context: cmd/tk/cmd/create.go wires --blocked-by/-b via a string flag parsed with splitCSV into BlockedBy (see createBlockedBy, flag registration ~line 81, struct fill ~line 194). cmd/tk/cmd/update.go updates tick fields. cmd/tk/cmd/show.go renders a "Blocked by:" line with per-blocker closed/open styling (~lines 69 and 132).

Work:
- tk create: add --after (comma-separated, no shorthand) -> sets After, same splitCSV parsing as --blocked-by.
- tk update: add --after to replace the whole list; explicit empty value (--after "") clears the field. Follow the existing convention in update.go for clearable flags.
- tk show: render an "After:" line directly beneath "Blocked by:" using the same style helpers and the same per-target closed/open glyph treatment; omit the line when the field is empty.
- Help text for both flags: "soft ordering: prefer after these ticks, but do not block on them".

Test cases (extend cmd/tk/cmd tests):
- tk create x --after a1,b2 -> stored tick has after ["a1","b2"].
- tk update <id> --after c3 -> after ["c3"]; then tk update <id> --after "" -> after omitted.
- tk show <id> with after set -> output contains "After:" and the ids; without -> no After line.

Files likely touched: cmd/tk/cmd/create.go, cmd/tk/cmd/update.go, cmd/tk/cmd/show.go, matching _test.go files.
Run: go test ./cmd/tk/...
acceptance_criteria: --after works on create and update (set and clear); tk show renders an After line; go test ./cmd/tk/... passes
